gaps.snippet: # [doc = " A typed predicate set describing in which [`CertMode`] tiers a property is expected to hold."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " This is the first-class representation of the cross-mode scope declaration from RFC-0034 §13:"] # [doc = " a test/suite *declares its intended scope*, and the harness checks both directions — the"] # [doc = " property fires where it applies **and** is correctly absent where it does not."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " ## Predefined scopes (the common RFC-0034 cases)"] # [doc = " - [`ALL_MODES`](ModeScope::ALL_MODES): property holds in every mode (e.g. never-silent"] # [doc = "   fallibility, cert_mode tag presence — Axis-B invariants)."] # [doc = " - [`FAST_ONLY`](ModeScope::FAST_ONLY): property holds only in `Fast` (e.g. the guarantee"] # [doc = "   floor from `Proven`/`Empirical` to `Declared`, cert suppression)."] # [doc = " - [`NON_FAST`](ModeScope::NON_FAST): `Balanced` + `Certified` — modes where the certification"] # [doc = "   machinery runs and `Empirical`/`Proven` tags are reachable (RFC-0034 §5). Alias:"] # [doc = "   [`EMIT_MODES`](ModeScope::EMIT_MODES)."] # [doc = " - [`CERTIFIED_ONLY`](ModeScope::CERTIFIED_ONLY): property holds only in `Certified` (e.g."] # [doc = "   certificate *checking*)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " ## Custom scopes"] # [doc = " Build with `ModeScope { in_scope: [fast_in, balanced_in, certified_in] }` — the bool array"] # [doc = " is indexed `[Fast=0, Balanced=1, Certified=2]`, matching [`CertMode::ALL`] order."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " ## Scope × scope composition"] # [doc = " `ModeScope` is `Copy` and composable: `ModeScope::union` / `ModeScope::intersect` let you build"] # [doc = " derived scopes without naming all booleans."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Declared`"] # [doc = " A `ModeScope` is a declaration, not a checked theorem. The harness checks conformance against"] # [doc = " the declared scope, but whether the scope is the *right* scope for the property is the test"] # [doc = " author's responsibility."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , Copy , PartialEq , Eq)] pub struct ModeScope { # [doc = " `in_scope[i]` = true means `CertMode::ALL[i]` is in scope. Indices: 0=Fast, 1=Balanced,"] # [doc = " 2=Certified — the same order as [`CertMode::ALL`] (Fast `depth()=0` … Certified `depth()=2`)."] pub in_scope : [bool ; 3] , }
gaps.reason: struct `ModeScope` has a field type with no confirmed mapping (unsupported Rust type form `[bool ; 3]`)

gaps.snippet: impl ModeScope { # [doc = " Property holds in **every** mode — the unconditional scope (e.g. Axis-B never-silent"] # [doc = " fallibility, cert_mode tag presence). In scope: Fast, Balanced, Certified."] pub const ALL_MODES : ModeScope = ModeScope { in_scope : [true , true , true] , } ; # [doc = " Property holds **only in `Fast`** (e.g. the floor from `Proven`/`Empirical` to `Declared`,"] # [doc = " cert suppression in Fast). In scope: Fast only."] pub const FAST_ONLY : ModeScope = ModeScope { in_scope : [true , false , false] , } ; # [doc = " Property holds in **`Balanced` and `Certified`** — the modes where the certification"] # [doc = " machinery runs and `Empirical`/`Proven` tags are reachable (RFC-0034 §5). Alias:"] # [doc = " [`EMIT_MODES`](ModeScope::EMIT_MODES). In scope: Balanced, Certified."] pub const NON_FAST : ModeScope = ModeScope { in_scope : [false , true , true] , } ; # [doc = " Property holds **only in `Certified`** (e.g. certificate *checking*, RFC-0034 §5 Axis-C)."] # [doc = " In scope: Certified only."] pub const CERTIFIED_ONLY : ModeScope = ModeScope { in_scope : [false , false , true] , } ; # [doc = " Property holds in **`Balanced` and `Certified`** — the modes that *emit* swap certificates"] # [doc = " (RFC-0034 §5). Alias for [`NON_FAST`](ModeScope::NON_FAST). In scope: Balanced, Certified."] pub const EMIT_MODES : ModeScope = ModeScope { in_scope : [false , true , true] , } ; # [doc = " Property holds only in `Balanced` (e.g. a balanced-specific cost/precision tradeoff)."] # [doc = " In scope: Balanced only."] pub const BALANCED_ONLY : ModeScope = ModeScope { in_scope : [false , true , false] , } ; # [doc = " Returns `true` iff the given mode is in scope."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact`"] # [doc = " # Fallibility: total"] # [must_use] pub fn contains (self , mode : CertMode) -> bool { self . in_scope [mode . depth () as usize] } # [doc = " The set of modes **in** scope (at most 3 elements, in `CertMode::ALL` order)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact`"] # [doc = " # Fallibility: total (always returns a `Vec` of 0..=3 elements)"] # [must_use] pub fn modes_in_scope (self) -> Vec < CertMode > { CertMode :: ALL . iter () . copied () . filter (| & m | self . contains (m)) . collect () } # [doc = " The set of modes **outside** scope (the complement; at most 3 elements)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact`"] # [doc = " # Fallibility: total"] # [must_use] pub fn modes_out_of_scope (self) -> Vec < CertMode > { CertMode :: ALL . iter () . copied () . filter (| & m | ! self . contains (m)) . collect () } # [doc = " The number of modes in scope (0..=3)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact`"] # [must_use] pub fn count (self) -> usize { self . in_scope . iter () . filter (| & & b | b) . count () } # [doc = " `true` iff the scope is empty (no modes — a scope with nothing in it is a no-op but"] # [doc = " surfaced explicitly, not silently ignored — C1/G2)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact`"] # [must_use] pub fn is_empty (self) -> bool { self . count () == 0 } # [doc = " The union of two scopes: a mode is in scope if it is in *either* scope."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact`"] # [doc = " # Fallibility: total"] # [must_use] pub fn union (self , other : ModeScope) -> ModeScope { ModeScope { in_scope : [self . in_scope [0] || other . in_scope [0] , self . in_scope [1] || other . in_scope [1] , self . in_scope [2] || other . in_scope [2] ,] , } } # [doc = " The intersection of two scopes: a mode is in scope only if it is in *both* scopes."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact`"] # [doc = " # Fallibility: total"] # [must_use] pub fn intersect (self , other : ModeScope) -> ModeScope { ModeScope { in_scope : [self . in_scope [0] && other . in_scope [0] , self . in_scope [1] && other . in_scope [1] , self . in_scope [2] && other . in_scope [2] ,] , } } # [doc = " Build a `ModeScope` from the effective mode of a [`ResolvedMode`]: the scope is \"exactly"] # [doc = " the modes at or above the resolved depth\" — i.e., `Fast` resolves to `ALL_MODES`,"] # [doc = " `Balanced` to `NON_FAST`, `Certified` to `CERTIFIED_ONLY`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " This is the bridge between the `@certification` resolver and the `ModeScope` predicate:"] # [doc = " a `@certification: balanced` declaration means \"this test cares about Balanced and"] # [doc = " Certified behaviour\", not \"this test only runs in Balanced\"."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact`"] # [doc = " # Fallibility: total"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **Mutant-witness:** if `from_resolved_mode(Balanced)` returned `CERTIFIED_ONLY`, a"] # [doc = " Balanced-scoped test would fail to check Balanced behaviour, and `modes_in_scope()` would"] # [doc = " return `[Certified]` instead of `[Balanced, Certified]`."] # [must_use] pub fn from_resolved_mode (r : & ResolvedMode) -> ModeScope { match r . mode { CertMode :: Fast => ModeScope :: ALL_MODES , CertMode :: Balanced => ModeScope :: NON_FAST , CertMode :: Certified => ModeScope :: CERTIFIED_ONLY , } } }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Configurable per-test / per-suite mode scope, resolved most-specific-wins via the shared"] # [doc = " M-790 `resolve_mode` algorithm (RFC-0034 §6 / RFC-0012 ambient-override)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The scope is built from a stack of `@certification` declarations at `Global`, `Phylum`,"] # [doc = " and `Nodule` scopes — the same three-tier lattice the `@certification` attribute uses in"] # [doc = " production code — plus an optional **granular** override that wins over all scope tiers."] # [doc = " Resolution uses [`mycelium_proj::resolve_mode`] directly: the test scope and the production"] # [doc = " `@certification` attribute share one algorithm (no parallel implementation, no drift — KC-3)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " ## Building a config"] # [doc = " ```rust"] # [doc = " # use mycelium_std_testing::cert_mode_test::ModeTestConfig;"] # [doc = " # use mycelium_core::cert_mode::CertMode;"] # [doc = " # use mycelium_proj::{CertDecl, CertScope};"] # [doc = " // Project default: fast (standard for most tests)."] # [doc = " let default_config = ModeTestConfig::default();"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " // Project-wide default of Balanced, overridden per-nodule to Certified:"] # [doc = " let config = ModeTestConfig::new(&["] # [doc = "     CertDecl { scope: CertScope::Phylum, mode: CertMode::Balanced },"] # [doc = "     CertDecl { scope: CertScope::Nodule,  mode: CertMode::Certified },"] # [doc = " ]);"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " // Granular per-test override — wins over all scope tiers:"] # [doc = " let narrow = config.with_granular(CertMode::Fast);"] # [doc = " ```"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " ## Resolving to a `ModeScope`"] # [doc = " [`ModeTestConfig::resolve`] runs `resolve_mode` and maps the effective mode to a `ModeScope`"] # [doc = " via [`ModeScope::from_resolved_mode`]."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " ## Provenance"] # [doc = " [`ModeTestConfig::provenance`] returns the `ResolvedMode` — the effective mode + which scope"] # [doc = " it came from — so the active test scope is never ambient (G2 / RFC-0012 renderability)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Declared` — a declaration, not a checked theorem (VR-5)"] # [derive (Debug , Clone)] pub struct ModeTestConfig { # [doc = " The `@certification` declarations at `Global`, `Phylum`, or `Nodule` scope. The resolver"] # [doc = " picks the most-specific winner; `Global` < `Phylum` < `Nodule` (RFC-0034 §6 lattice)."] decls : Vec < CertDecl > , # [doc = " An optional **granular** override that wins over all scope tiers — the per-test dial."] # [doc = " `None` = no granular override (falls back to the `decls` resolution)."] granular : Option < CertMode > , }

gaps.snippet: impl ModeTestConfig { # [doc = " Build a `ModeTestConfig` from a slice of `@certification` scope declarations."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Declarations are not ordered (the resolver picks the highest-specificity winner). Supply"] # [doc = " at most one per scope level (duplicates at the same scope are resolved by"] # [doc = " `resolve_mode`'s max-specificity fold — the **last** duplicate found wins, per"] # [doc = " `Iterator::max_by_key`'s documented tie behaviour (\"if several elements are equally"] # [doc = " maximum, the last element is returned\"); in practice, the `@certification` parser"] # [doc = " upstream rejects duplicates, so this is just the resolver's behaviour for robustness)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact` (pure construction)"] # [doc = " # Fallibility: total"] # [must_use] pub fn new (decls : & [CertDecl]) -> Self { ModeTestConfig { decls : decls . to_vec () , granular : None , } } # [doc = " Add (or replace) a **granular** per-test override — the most-specific tier, overrides"] # [doc = " all scope declarations in [`ModeTestConfig::new`]."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact`"] # [doc = " # Fallibility: total"] # [must_use] pub fn with_granular (mut self , mode : CertMode) -> Self { self . granular = Some (mode) ; self } # [doc = " The provenance of the resolved test mode — the effective mode and the scope it came from."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `source: None` means no declaration matched and the project default"] # [doc = " ([`CertMode::default`] = `Fast`) is in effect. `source: Some(CertScope::Nodule)` means a"] # [doc = " nodule-wide header won. When [`with_granular`](ModeTestConfig::with_granular) is set, the"] # [doc = " provenance reflects the granular override with `source: None` (the granular tier is not"] # [doc = " a `CertScope` level — it is above the lattice and wins unconditionally)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The provenance is available for `EXPLAIN` rendering: a test that does not know which tiers"] # [doc = " ran can inspect the `ResolvedMode` and surface the scope decision (never ambient — G2)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact`"] # [doc = " # Fallibility: total"] # [must_use] pub fn provenance (& self) -> ResolvedMode { if let Some (g) = self . granular { return ResolvedMode { mode : g , source : None , } ; } resolve_mode (& self . decls) } # [doc = " Resolve the effective `ModeScope` for this test/suite configuration."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Uses [`ModeScope::from_resolved_mode`] on the [`provenance`](ModeTestConfig::provenance)"] # [doc = " result — so the mapping from `@certification` mode to test scope is canonical and shared."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact` (pure, deterministic)"] # [doc = " # Fallibility: total"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **EXPLAIN:** call [`ModeTestConfig::provenance`] on the same config to see which scope"] # [doc = " tier produced the effective mode (never ambient — G2 / RFC-0012 renderability)."] # [must_use] pub fn resolve (& self) -> ModeScope { ModeScope :: from_resolved_mode (& self . provenance ()) } }

gaps.snippet: impl Default for ModeTestConfig { # [doc = " Default configuration: no declarations → project default `Fast` → `ALL_MODES` scope."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " This is the sensible default (RFC-0034 §13: \"a mode-sensitive unit covers the tiers whose"] # [doc = " behaviour differs, plus the negatives\"): a test with no `@certification` context runs"] # [doc = " across all tiers. Widening from `fast` is always safe."] fn default () -> Self { ModeTestConfig { decls : vec ! [] , granular : None , } } }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " A summary of which `CertMode` tiers were visited and which were skipped by"] # [doc = " [`for_each_mode_in`]."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Carries the never-silent audit: the caller can assert that the expected tiers ran and the"] # [doc = " right tiers were skipped. A test that only \"happened to\" cover the in-scope modes without"] # [doc = " explicitly checking the out-of-scope modes gets no false confidence (C1/G2)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Declared` — a record of what ran; not a verdict on correctness."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq , Eq)] pub struct ModeVisit { # [doc = " The modes that were visited (in `CertMode::ALL` order)."] pub visited : Vec < CertMode > , # [doc = " The modes that were skipped because they were outside the scope (in `CertMode::ALL` order)."] pub skipped : Vec < CertMode > , }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Run `f(mode)` for **every** mode in [`CertMode::ALL`] (weakest → strongest: Fast, Balanced,"] # [doc = " Certified)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The simplest entry point for mode-parametric tests: the property is checked across the full"] # [doc = " tier sweep. Use [`for_each_mode_in`] if you need scope filtering; use [`assert_mode_scope`]"] # [doc = " if you also need the cross-mode negative check."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Never-silent: all three modes are always visited. There is no way to \"skip a mode\" through"] # [doc = " this function (use `ModeScope` + `for_each_mode_in` for that)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact` (visits exactly the three modes in `CertMode::ALL` order)"] # [doc = " # Fallibility: total (panics only if `f` panics)"] # [doc = " # Effects: none (pure; the side-effects live in `f`)"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # EXPLAIN"] # [doc = " The visited tiers are `CertMode::ALL` — always `[Fast, Balanced, Certified]`. No filtering."] pub fn for_each_mode (mut f : impl FnMut (CertMode)) { for & mode in & CertMode :: ALL { f (mode) ; } }

# [doc = " Run `f(mode)` for each mode **in** `scope`, returning a [`ModeVisit`] that records which"] # [doc = " modes were visited and which were skipped."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The caller can assert `visit.matches_scope(scope)` to confirm the right set ran, and inspect"] # [doc = " `visit.skipped` to see which modes were out of scope (never silently absent — C1/G2)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact`"] # [doc = " # Fallibility: total (panics only if `f` panics)"] # [doc = " # Effects: none"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # EXPLAIN"] # [doc = " `ModeVisit` carries the full visited/skipped split — the caller can inspect it at any"] # [doc = " verbosity level. The split is deterministic: `scope` fully determines which modes are in"] # [doc = " `visited` vs `skipped`."] pub fn for_each_mode_in (scope : ModeScope , mut f : impl FnMut (CertMode)) -> ModeVisit { let mut visited = Vec :: new () ; let mut skipped = Vec :: new () ; for & mode in & CertMode :: ALL { if scope . contains (mode) { f (mode) ; visited . push (mode) ; } else { skipped . push (mode) ; } } ModeVisit { visited , skipped } }

gaps.snippet:
# [doc = " Assert that `predicate(mode)` returns **`false`** for every mode **outside** `scope` — the"] # [doc = " negative-only half of the cross-mode negative pattern."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Use this when you want to assert the **absence** of a property in the out-of-scope modes,"] # [doc = " without asserting the positive direction (you may have a separate positive check, e.g. the"] # [doc = " standard `assert!` in a mode-pinned test body). This is the \"invariant must be absent where"] # [doc = " it doesn't apply\" helper — the dual of a simple positive assertion."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact`"] # [doc = " # Fallibility: panics when `predicate(mode)` returns `true` for a mode outside scope."] # [doc = " # Effects: none"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # EXPLAIN"] # [doc = " The panic carries the violating mode and the scope declaration (C3)."] pub fn assert_mode_negative (scope : ModeScope , predicate : impl Fn (CertMode) -> bool , desc : & str) { for & mode in & CertMode :: ALL { if ! scope . contains (mode) && predicate (mode) { panic ! ("cross-mode NEGATIVE failed: `{desc}` holds in {mode:?} but scope={scope:?} \
                 says it should NOT (property present outside its declared scope — RFC-0034 §13).") ; } } }
